title: Boat Rental in St Barts: Costs, Tips and Top Routes for 2026
html:
St Barts offers some of the most exclusive yachting in the Caribbean, with departures from Gustavia harbour and half-day motorboat charters starting around €600. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators so you can compare options and book with confidence. Whether you want to explore secluded coves or host a party on a catamaran, planning ahead ensures a smooth experience.

Where to set off in St Barts

Gustavia harbour is the island's main departure point, with several marinas and piers for short-term berthing. From here you can head to the anchorage at Shell Beach or circumnavigate the island via Colombier Beach, which is only accessible by boat. Other good launch spots include Saint-Jean Bay, where the airport runway sits alongside the water, and the sheltered cove at Grand Cul-de-Sac. If you want to explore beyond the main island, consider a trip to Île Fourchue or the nearby islets off Anse des Cayes. Most charter operators will suggest a route based on wind and swell for the day.

Which boat to rent

Choose a vessel that suits your group size and itinerary, with prices from €350 per half day.

  1. Small motorboat (20-25ft), best for couples or small families wanting to explore quiet coves; up to 4 guests, from €350 for a half day.
  2. Day cruiser (28-32ft like the Axopar Limpopo), great for 4-6 guests who want a fast, comfortable ride with a sun pad; from €600 half day. See the Limpopo listing for full specs and availability.
  3. Sailing catamaran (38-45ft), ideal for 8-10 guests on a full-day cruise with snorkelling stops; from €1,200 full day.
  4. Crewed luxury yacht (50ft+, e.g. Maiora or Azimut), for a premium experience with a captain and host, up to 12 guests; from €3,000 full day.
  5. RIB or tender, handy for watersports and getting into shallow anchorages; up to 6 guests, from €400 half day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in St Barts?

Prices vary widely by boat type, season and duration, with shared tours from €150 per person and private charters starting at €600. To get an accurate picture for your dates, you can compare verified boat listings on yacht charter St Barts platforms that let you filter by price, capacity and amenities.

  • Shared group tour (e.g. catamaran excursion): €150-€250 per person for 4 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4-6 guests): €350-€700.
  • Catamaran full-day (8-12 guests): €1,200-€2,500.
  • Luxury crewed yacht full-day (up to 12 guests): €3,000-€6,000.

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in St Barts?

If you intend to skipper the boat yourself, you may need a valid boating licence or an International Certificate of Competence (ICC). French regulations apply in St Barts, and many operators require proof of experience for bareboat charters. However, most visitors opt for a crewed charter where a professional captain handles the navigation, which removes any licence concerns. Always confirm with the rental operator before booking, as policies vary.

For the best conditions, book between December and April when the trade winds are steady and the sea is calm around the leeward anchorages.

When is the best season to rent a boat in St Barts?

The peak season runs from December to April, offering settled weather and consistent winds such as the north-east trade winds. During these months, the sea is generally calmer, especially on the leeward side of the island, making it easier to explore anchorages like Anse de Colombier. May to November is the low season with higher humidity and a chance of tropical storms, though prices can be lower. Many charter companies operate year-round, but you may find fewer boat options in the quieter months.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150 (shared) to €6,000 (luxury yacht) per day
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Typical trip length: half day (4 hours) or full day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on most private charters
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in St Barts

Can I book a boat for a single day in St Barts?

Yes, most charters offer half-day or full-day rentals, with many operators accepting last-minute bookings, though advance reservation is recommended during the high season.

What is the best way to see St Barts from the water?

A half-day tour that includes Gustavia, Colombier Beach and Île Fourchue covers the main sights and allows for swimming and snorkelling stops.

Are there any hidden fees when renting a boat in St Barts?

Always check if the quoted price includes fuel, crew, taxes and any docking fees, as some operators add these on top, especially for luxury yachts.

How far is it from Gustavia to Colombier Beach by boat?

The distance is about 2 nautical miles, a quick 15-minute trip in a motorboat or 30 minutes under sail with a steady breeze.

Whether you are a seasoned skipper or a first-time renter, St Barts offers unforgettable boating experiences. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in St Barts or list your own. If you own a charter boat, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach travellers planning their 2026 holiday.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in St Barts and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
